title: Ozoroa cinerea
intro: Ozoroa cinerea, commonly known as the Grey Milkwood, is a tree species found in southern Africa. The Grey Milkwood tree has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ozoroa cinerea include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ozoroa cinerea is often made into a decoction or a tincture, which is used to treat various health conditions, and it is also sometimes used in combination with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Ozoroa cinerea is classified within the family Anacardiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the cashew or poison ivy family. This family consists of approximately 83 genera and over 600 species. Ozoroa cinerea belongs to the genus Ozoroa, which is a group of flowering plants native to southern Africa. The genus Ozoroa is divided into around 20 species, with Ozoroa cinerea being one of the species within this group. The classification of Ozoroa cinerea reflects its evolutionary history and close relationship with other plants within the Anacardiaceae family.
